title: Bridge Networks, DNS, Ports, Routing, IPv6, and Exposure Policy · Delivery Case: Docker Internal Multinetwork Segmentation
summary: A multi-service application works through localhost on one machine but fails by service name and accidentally exposes its database to every interface. Planned artifact: Add and verify the docker internal multinetwork segmentation increment in least-exposure container network topology. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Segment frontend, backend, data, egress, internal, and attachable networks and verify which paths remain reachable.
Distinguish listening, EXPOSE, expose, publish, host address, host port, container port, and direct-routing behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Predict Docker Internal Multinetwork Segmentation · Theory Docker Internal Multinetwork Segmentation
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Compose stack publishes its database on every host interface, pins a container IP, and calls localhost from the API. Split trust networks, use service DNS, restrict publishing, and verify ingress separately from egress.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Docker Internal Multinetwork Segmentation contract for the supplied data.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example Compose stack publishes its database on every host interface, pins a container IP, and calls localhost from the API. Split trust networks, use service DNS, restrict publishing, and verify ingress separately from egress. Competency probe: Segment frontend, backend, data, egress, internal, and attachable networks and verify which paths remain reachable. A defensible solution applies Docker Internal Multinetwork Segmentation, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to least-exposure container network topology.

content.code:
# Worked focus: docker-internal-multinetwork-segmentation — Segment frontend, backend, data, egress, internal, and attachable networks and verify which paths remain reachable.
  # Evidence: docker-worked-docker-networking-dns-publishing-5
  - competency: docker-worked-docker-networking-dns-publishing-5
    artifact: network-contract.yaml
    decision: worked docker networking dns publishing 5
    scenario-variant: 4
    environment: ephemeral development host with constrained disk and memory
    fault-injection: rebuild after the approved base digest is withdrawn
    retained-proof: accessible recovery runbook with rollback rehearsal timing
    case-key: worked5
    change: |
      frontend-network: published-edge
      backend-network: internal
      service-discovery: service-name
      host-binding: 127.0.0.1:8080
      database-published: false
    verify: docker network inspect intake-backend --format json
    failure: Reject container IP pinning, localhost-as-peer assumptions, accidental all-interface publishing, or an exposed database port.
    repair: Use project-scoped DNS names, separate networks by trust, bind only the required host interface, and test egress and ingress independently.
content.caption: Runnable Docker artifact and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Multiple Compose networks automatically enforce application-layer authorization. This fails the Docker Internal Multinetwork Segmentation contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Segment frontend, backend, data, egress, internal, and attachable networks and verify which paths remain reachable.
